description: Recto (secondary use): Complete letter from ʿEzra to Seʿadya. In Judaeo-Arabic. Written on a thin strip of paper, with sharply upslanting lines. Dating: Perhaps 13th or 14th century. The sender apologizes for the delay and is anxious to convey his eagerness to serve, lest 'Sayyidnā' be upset with him like last time. (Information in part from CUDL.)
